report_name: 'The Gentlemen' Ransomware Group Emerges as a Top-Tier Threat with Advanced TTPs

threat_actors.name: The Gentlemen
threat_actors.description: A sophisticated Russian-speaking Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) group that emerged in mid-2025. By April 2026, it was ranked as the second most active ransomware operation, responsible for 10% of global attacks. The group's leadership is believed to have past ties to the Qilin ransomware program.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through a double-extortion ransomware model.

threat_actors.name: Qilin
threat_actors.description: An established ransomware program with which the leader of 'The Gentlemen' reportedly has past ties.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Used by The Gentlemen's RaaS affiliates as a typical initial access vector.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Used by The Gentlemen's RaaS affiliates to gain initial access into victim networks.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Used by The Gentlemen's RaaS affiliates in the form of stolen credentials for initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Proxy
attack_patterns.description: The SystemBC RAT turns an infected host into a SOCKS5 proxy, allowing The Gentlemen affiliates to route their traffic through the compromised machine to hide their C2 infrastructure.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The core ransomware payload encrypts files on Windows, Linux, and VMware ESXi systems as part of the group's double extortion model.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: Sensitive data is exfiltrated from victim networks before encryption, with the threat of public release used to pressure victims into paying the ransom.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: Affiliates were observed using 'vssadmin.exe delete shadows' to prevent easy recovery from encrypted files.

malware.name: The Gentlemen Ransomware
malware.description: A multi-platform ransomware payload supporting Windows, Linux, BSD, and VMware ESXi. It uses XChaCha20 and Curve25519 for encryption and is distributed via a Ransomware-as-a-Service model by 'The Gentlemen' group.

malware.name: SystemBC
malware.description: A remote access trojan (RAT) deployed by The Gentlemen affiliates. It functions as a SOCKS5 proxy on the infected host, enabling attackers to obfuscate C2 communications and facilitate lateral movement.

identities.name: UK software consultancy
identities.description: A victim of The Gentlemen ransomware group, whose compromise was used as a pressure tactic against one of its clients, demonstrating the group's understanding of supply chain dynamics.

identities.name: NCC Group
identities.description: A cybersecurity research firm that ranked The Gentlemen as the second most active ransomware operation in April 2026.

infrastructure.name: SystemBC C2 Proxy Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Compromised victim machines turned into SOCKS5 proxies by the SystemBC RAT to tunnel and obfuscate C2 traffic.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 2b9a8c7d-1e3f-4b6a-9c8d-0f1e2d3c4b5a
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common precursor to ransomware deployment as observed with 'The Gentlemen' group.
references:
  - https://www.computerweekly.com/news/366585108/The-Gentlemen-emerging-as-key-ransomware-player
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-28
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a technique used by The Gentlemen affiliates to inhibit system recovery.

indicators.name: The Gentlemen Ransomware File Encryption Pattern
indicators.pattern:
title: The Gentlemen Ransomware File Encryption Pattern
id: 5d6e7f8a-9b0c-4d1e-8f9a-0b1c2d3e4f5a
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of files with the '.cha' extension, a potential indicator of The Gentlemen ransomware encrypting files on a host.
references:
  - https://www.computerweekly.com/news/366585108/The-Gentlemen-emerging-as-key-ransomware-player
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-28
tags:
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.impact
level: critical
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.cha'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the mass creation of files with a uniform, non-standard extension (e.g., .cha), which is indicative of The Gentlemen ransomware activity.

relationships.source_name: The Gentlemen Ransomware File Encryption Pattern
